response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates impressive software engineering breadth with 15+ years of experience across multiple languages and platforms, this role requires deep expertise in embedded firmware security and networking—areas entirely absent from his background. Most critically, native-level Japanese proficiency is a required qualification with zero evidence of competency; his resume lists only English (fluent), Romanian (fluent), and German (basic). His recent work (past 10 years) has focused exclusively on web and cloud platforms (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB), while his embedded C/C++ experience at National Instruments ended in 2015. For a mid-senior individual contributor role expecting immediate expertise in security technologies, LAN-switch products, and Linux kernel analysis, the domain gap and language barrier make this unsuitable.
strengths: 15+ years of proven software engineering experience with strong fundamentals across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems showing ability to learn diverse domains
Hands-on experience with C, C++, Linux systems, and infrastructure tooling (GitLab, CI/CD, Docker); organized technical workshops demonstrating knowledge-sharing ability
Demonstrated excellence in code quality and software craftsmanship with TDD, clean architecture, and mentoring through multiple senior and staff-level roles
concerns: No evidence of native-level Japanese language proficiency, which is explicitly listed as a required qualification and essential for this role
Recent 10-year focus on web/cloud platforms (TypeScript, Node.js, React, AWS CDK) with no embedded systems or security engineering; C/embedded experience is from 2014-2015 and severely outdated

No demonstrated expertise in required security domains (encryption, key exchange, MACsec, 802.1X, authentication) or embedded networking (Layer 2/3, switch operation, NETCONF, SNMP) or Linux kernel source analysis
